Based on the detailed review of the evidence provided regarding the carotid sinus and its location, we observe significant variability in its positioning despite being typically found near the bifurcation of the common carotid artery into internal and external components. Specifically, the studies highlight four main locations ranging from starting immediately after the common carotid artery splits to extending further downstream toward various points depending on individual morphology. Given this substantial range of potential origins, our earlier assertion simplistically stating 'it initiates around the division site of the common carotid artery' needs qualification considering variability noted.
